input.content:
Feature: Week number formatting in FastDateFormat
  As a user of FastDateFormat
  I want to ensure that week numbers are formatted correctly according to locale-specific rules
  So that dates are properly assigned to weeks based on the locale's definition of week numbering

  Scenario: Format week number for January 1, 2010 in Swedish locale
    Given a date of January 1, 2010 (which is a Friday)
    And a Swedish locale (sv_SE) is used
    When the date is formatted with pattern "EEEE', week 'ww"
    Then the result should be "fredag, week 53"
    And not "fredag, week 1"
    And this reflects that January 1, 2010 belongs to the last week of 2009 according to Swedish week numbering rules

  Scenario: Week number calculation respects locale-specific first week rules
    Given a FastDateFormat instance with pattern containing 'w' (week in year)
    And a specific locale that defines first week differently (e.g. Swedish week 1 must contain a Thursday)
    When formatting dates near year boundaries
    Then the week number should follow the locale's specific rules for determining the first week of the year
    And not rely on default locale-agnostic calendar calculations

content.content: Successfully created and wrote to new file: /Users/nothing/workspace/prometheus_workdir_qwen/lang_26_architect/requirement.feature.

content.text:
I've analyzed the failing test case `testLang645` and created the requirement.feature file. The issue is related to locale-specific week number calculations in the FastDateFormat class. Let me explain what I found:

The failing test `testLang645` expects that January 1, 2010 formatted as "EEEE', week 'ww" in Swedish locale should produce "fredag, week 53" (where "fredag" means Friday in Swedish). This indicates that according to Swedish calendar rules, January 1, 2010 falls in the 53rd week of 2009, not the 1st week of 2010.

The problem stems from the fact that different locales have different rules for determining the first week of the year. In Sweden (and many European countries), the first week of the year is typically defined as the week that contains at least 4 days of the new year, or the week that contains January 4th, or the week that contains the first Thursday. This means January 1st can sometimes fall in the last week of the previous year.

I have successfully created the requirement.feature file that captures this expected behavior and addresses the specific bug. I will now stop as instructed.
